normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_length (adj : Adj) (source : Nat) : (bfsShortestPath adj source).length = adj.length := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance table has the same length as the adjacency list (aligned with the number of vertices).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_source_anchor (adj : Adj) (source : Nat) :
  (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? source = some (some 0) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is exactly 0 edges.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_reachability_witness (adj : Adj) (source v k : Nat) :
  (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some (some k) →
    ∃ path : List Nat,
      path.length = k + 1 ∧
      path.get? 0 = some source ∧
      path.get? (k) = some v ∧
      (∀ i : Nat, i < k →
        match path.get? i, path.get? (i+1) with
        | some a, some b => b ∈ (adj.get? a).getD []
        | _, _ => False) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), then there exists a directed path from source to v using exactly k edges (k+1 vertices).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_minimality (adj : Adj) (source v k : Nat) :
  (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some (some k) →
    ¬ (∃ path : List Nat,
        path.length = (k) ∧
        path.get? 0 = some source ∧
        path.get? (k-1) = some v ∧
        (∀ i : Nat, i + 1 < k →
          match path.get? i, path.get? (i+1) with
          | some a, some b => b ∈ (adj.get? a).getD []
          | _, _ => False)) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(k), then there is no directed path from source to v using fewer than k edges.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_unreachability_iff (adj : Adj) (source v : Nat) :
  (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some none ↔
    ¬ (∃ k : Nat, (bfsShortestPath adj source).get? v = some (some k)) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: dist[v] = None exactly when there is no k such that dist[v] = Some(k).
